domain: Aztec atlatl spear-thrower (pre-Columbian Mesoamerican weapon): low-mass rod 50-60cm functions as ARM EXTENSION lever; 2-6x mechanical advantage in projectile velocity; rod adds no energy, just lengthens lever; force applied over longer distance = higher tip velocity.
specific_mechanism: Passive lever extension multiplies useful force without adding source energy; making moving arm longer creates higher tip velocity via v = ω · r.
llm_application: ATLATL-LEVER gradient leverage via learned CoT extension policy: during RLVR fine-tune, model can OPTIONALLY append k extra reasoning tokens before final answer. Final-answer correctness signal back-propagates through ENTIRE chain (long CoT included). Gradient signal at early tokens multiplied by lever ratio (1 + k/L_base). Small swing (gradient at first step) produces large tip velocity (final-answer gradient via long chain). Differs from (a) standard CoT (handcrafted not learned), (b) DeepSeek-R1 RLVR (no lever-length policy), (c) o1 (implicit length scaling), (d) Long-CoT 2502.03373 (analyzes not lever-policy) by combining (i) LEARNED EXTENSION POLICY + (ii) PROPORTIONAL gradient leverage through chain + (iii) MECHANICAL-ADVANTAGE RATIO as hyperparameter.
novelty_claim: Standard CoT handcrafted. RLVR no lever-length policy. o1 implicit scaling. Long-CoT analysis. ATLATL-LEVER recombines (a) LEARNED extension policy + (b) PROPORTIONAL gradient leverage + (c) MECHANICAL-ADVANTAGE ratio hyperparameter. Three known components combined; not wholly new.
content_words: learned CoT extension policy gradient leverage
proportional gradient amplification through chain length

llm_side: learned CoT extension policy gradient leverage
proportional gradient amplification through chain length
